llcFilesWith: County clerk of the county of the principal place of business
soleProprietorFilesWith: County clerk of the county of the principal place of business
feeBasis: The statutory fee is $10 for the first name and owner plus $2 for each additional one (Bus. & Prof. Code § 17929), but Government Code § 54985 lets each county raise its fees to recover costs, and counties do: Placer County charges $30 for the first name. Publication is paid separately at the newspaper's rate.
renewalRule: Renewable for further 5-year periods. No republication is needed when nothing has changed and the renewal is filed within 40 days of expiry. A statement also expires 40 days after any change in its facts.
publicationRule: Within 45 days of filing, the statement runs in a newspaper of general circulation in the county once a week for four successive weeks, and an affidavit of publication is filed with the clerk within 45 days of the last run. Bus. & Prof. Code § 17917; Gov. Code § 6064.
protectionNote: California sits between the no-rights states and Ohio: filing first, while actually doing business under the name, creates a rebuttable presumption of the exclusive right to use it as a trade name in that county only (Bus. & Prof. Code § 14411). That is evidence for a dispute, not a statewide grant.
searchNote: There is no statewide fictitious business name search. Each county clerk keeps its own index, many of them online. The Secretary of State's bizfile search does not include FBN statements.
notes: Filing is due within 40 days of starting business under the name. A registrant with no California place of business files with the Clerk of Sacramento County. The four-week newspaper run is the longest publication requirement of the five pilot states.

sources.title: Presumption of exclusive right from filing
